title: Son la Mer
subtitle: Mauritius from the ocean's perspective.
lore_intro: The sea is how Mauritius welcomes you. River first, then bay, then reef. Three waters, three moods — this is the island before it was an island.
epilogue: You paddled the river at dawn, swam with spinner dolphins and drifted over Blue Bay's reef. The ocean has nothing left to hide from you. Ti Dodo nods: 'Now you know why we never leave.'

title: Randonner
subtitle: Walk the spine of the island.
lore_intro: Each trail harder than the last. Gorge to cascades to summit. The island tests you before it opens up — and what it reveals at the top is worth every step.
epilogue: Gorge, cascades, summit. Three different Mauritiuses, all reached on foot. Ti Dodo bows: 'The trails of this island will answer when you call.'

title: Moris Enn Sel
subtitle: The complete island arc.
lore_intro: You don't see Mauritius until you've seen it whole. Lagoon, ridge, gorge, open road, and a drum at sunset. This is the full map — and the people who made it.
epilogue: Dolphins at dawn. Summit at sunrise. Gorge at midday. Birds at dusk. Sega at night. You didn't visit Mauritius — you lived a week of it the way it's meant to be lived. Ti Dodo grins: 'You are one of us now.'

title: Island Pulse
subtitle: Port Louis, Sega, Creole table, local life.
lore_intro: Mauritius isn't just nature — it's nine cultures layered on a small island. This quest is the human map: the drum, the table, the market, the street. Come hungry.
epilogue: The Sega beat in your chest, the dholl puri on your tongue, the market noise still ringing. You have not just visited Mauritius — you have been welcomed into it.

title: Smooth Passage
subtitle: The trip that runs itself.
lore_intro: The island is easier when someone who knows it has your back. Plan it right, move through it smoothly, leave a trace. This quest is about using the full Andeor platform — not just the trails.
epilogue: Planned. Moved. Remembered. You used every tool the platform offers and the trip delivered. Ti Dodo: 'The best journeys leave nothing to chance and everything to wonder.'
